runtime_notes: OCR cross-check across all 8 pages: zero JP wrong-char / hangul / table-flatten / answer-line-loss / callout-misread defects observed in canonical ocr/ stage. JP-OCR track converged for this fresh sample.

Page 124 FAIL: released output/pages/page_124.md still contains duplicate-marker answer lines '> **Answer**: A. a. Cash Cow' and '> **Answer**: B. b. Product prices of one's own company' (q::0 and q::1). The choices arrays themselves were correctly normalized to A/B/C/D, but the markdown exporter's answer-text composer concatenated the legacy lowercase marker from pre-normalization choice text. Root cause is in the exporter, not in choices data.
Same dup-marker pattern detected (via grep) on 10+ other pages outside my sample: 042, 064, 125, 155, 182, 203, 205, 259, 261. This is a systemic exporter bug, NOT page-local.

Recommended fix: in markdown exporter normalize the answer-text exactly like choice text — strip leading [A-Da-d甲-丁ア-エ][.．、]\s* before prepending canonical 'A. ' prefix. Re-render output/pages/*.md, then verify with: grep -lnE '\\*\\*Answer\\*\\*: [A-D]\\. [a-d]\\.' output/pages/*.md  should return empty.

Page 113 WARN: canonical translated/ has overzealous '(PPM)' disambiguator appended to every quadrant term inside narrative prose and figure caption — heavy zh, en clean. Polish-only, no release defect.

Page 124 also has translation-stage polish: translated/page_124.json carries mixed ア-エ / 甲-丁 / lowercase a-d / uppercase A-D markers across the 4 questions on the same page (q::1 mixes ア/イ + 丙/丁; q::2 uses 甲乙丙丁; q::0 uses ア-エ in jp + lowercase a-d in en + A-D in zh-corrected). Programmatic export normalization currently masks this in output/ — but canonical translated/ is inconsistent. Polish-grade, not release-blocking on its own.
Page 67 has zero entities (translated:[]). Correct — page is past-exam answer-key narrative ('解答 1-12' through '解答 1-16') with no term/question/section/table boundaries to extract. Not a defect.

Page 69 emits only the chapter entity (法務). Intro bullets ('ここに注目して読もう！') and the 分野/大分類 syllabus mapping table on the OCR are NOT promoted to structured entities. Likely intentional chapter-cover scope rule; flagged as coverage note, not as defect.
Page 74 has a minor zh inconsistency: table row uses raw 「マル秘」 inside Chinese sentence whereas the term-card row glosses it. Polish-only.

R10 convergence status: JP-OCR track = CONVERGED (0 defects, 0 WARN on fresh sample). Translation track = NOT YET CONVERGED — one new release-impacting exporter bug (page 124 answer-line duplicate marker) discovered. Recommend R10-C: patch markdown exporter + re-render output/pages/*.md, then re-run R10 fresh-sample audit.
